message:
test(e2e): add comprehensive Table rendering E2E tests

Adds 37 E2E tests covering the Table renderable:
- Simple table scenarios (2x2, single column)
- Full features (title, caption, footer, row separators, ASCII box)
- Width constraints (fixed, min/max, expand, collapse)
- Wide characters (CJK, emoji, mixed-width)
- Cell wrapping and alignment (left, center, right)
- Edge cases (empty, sparse rows, no edges)
- Styled tables and alternating row styles
- 4 snapshot tests for visual regression

All tests verify rendering through the complete pipeline with
detailed tracing for debugging failures.

Closes: rich_rust-ehe

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"rich_rust-cwe","title":"Validate Unicode Cell Width against RICH_SPEC Section 12","description":"## Objective
Verify the Unicode cell width implementation in src/cells.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 12 exactly.

## Background
Terminals display characters with different widths: ASCII is 1 cell, CJK is 2 cells, some are 0 cells. Accurate width calculation is essential for table layout and text wrapping.

## Validation Checklist

### 12.1 Character Width Categories
- [ ] Verify ASCII (U+0020-U+007E): width 1
- [ ] Verify Control chars (U+0000-U+001F): width 0
- [ ] Verify CJK (various ranges): width 2
- [ ] Verify Emoji (with variation selectors): width 2
- [ ] Verify Combining marks: width 0
- [ ] Verify Zero-width joiners: width 0

### 12.2 Functions
- [ ] Verify get_character_cell_size(char) -> usize
- [ ] Verify cell_len(text) -> usize (sum of char widths)
- [ ] Verify set_cell_size_func() for custom width function (if implemented)

### 12.3 Unicode-Width Crate Usage
- [ ] Verify using unicode-width crate for width calculation
- [ ] Verify UnicodeWidthChar::width() is used
- [ ] Verify fallback for chars without width

### 12.4 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test emoji: single emoji, emoji with skin tone, emoji ZWJ sequences
- [ ] Test CJK: Chinese, Japanese, Korean characters
- [ ] Test combining: e + combining acute = 1 cell
- [ ] Test tabs: should be 0 or configurable
- [ ] Test newlines: width 0

### 12.5 Performance (if LRU cache implemented)
- [ ] Verify caching of width lookups
- [ ] Test cache hit ratio with typical text

## Files to Examine
- src/cells.rs (primary)
- Cargo.toml (unicode-width dependency)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 12","acceptance_criteria":"Width calculation matches spec for all Unicode categories. Edge cases handled.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:41.242340206-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:18:41.242340206-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-cwe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:38.448134401-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-de4","title":"Audit Error Handling Consistency","description":"## Objective
Audit all error handling to ensure consistency and helpful error messages.

## Background
Good error messages are critical for library usability. This task audits all error types and messages for consistency and helpfulness.

## Audit Checklist

### Error Types
- [ ] All modules use consistent error type pattern (thiserror recommended)
- [ ] Error types implement std::error::Error
- [ ] Error types implement Display with helpful messages
- [ ] Error types implement Debug

### Error Messages
- [ ] Messages explain WHAT went wrong
- [ ] Messages explain WHY it's an error
- [ ] Messages suggest HOW to fix (where applicable)
- [ ] No raw "unwrap failed" or "unexpected error"

### Example Good vs Bad
```rust
// BAD
Err(ColorError::InvalidColor)

// GOOD
Err(ColorError::InvalidColorName {
    name: name.to_string(),
    suggestion: Self::suggest_similar_color(name),
    help: "Valid colors are: red, green, blue, ... or use #RRGGBB hex format",
})
```

### Audit by Module
- [ ] color.rs - ColorError
- [ ] style.rs - StyleError
- [ ] markup/mod.rs - MarkupError
- [ ] text.rs - TextError (if any)
- [ ] console.rs - ConsoleError (if any)
- [ ] renderables/table.rs - TableError (if any)

### Recovery Behavior
- [ ] Markup parser: graceful degradation for invalid tags
- [ ] Color parser: clear error for invalid colors
- [ ] Style parser: clear error for invalid attributes
- [ ] Table: handle edge cases without panic

### Testing Error Paths
- [ ] All error variants have tests
- [ ] Error messages verified in tests
- [ ] Recovery behavior documented

## Files to Examine
- All files with Error types
- All uses of unwrap(), expect(), panic!()

## Deliverables
1. Audit report of current state
2. List of improvements needed
3. Consistency guidelines document","acceptance_criteria":"All error handling audited. Improvements documented. Guidelines established.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:47.91244963-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:47.91244963-05:00"}
 {"id":"rich_rust-e23","title":"Test Feature Flag Combinations","description":"Create comprehensive tests for all feature flag combinations to ensure no compilation or runtime issues.

FEATURE FLAGS (from Cargo.toml):
- default = []
- syntax = ["syntect"]
- markdown = ["pulldown-cmark"]
- json = ["serde_json"]
- full = ["syntax", "markdown", "json"]

COMBINATIONS TO TEST (2^3 = 8):
1. No features (default)
2. syntax only
3. markdown only
4. json only
5. syntax + markdown
6. syntax + json
7. markdown + json
8. full (all three)

TEST REQUIREMENTS:

1. COMPILATION TESTS:
   - All 8 combinations must compile without warnings
   - No unused import warnings in any combination
   - No missing dependency errors

2. CONDITIONAL EXPORT TESTS:
   - Verify prelude exports correct items per feature
   - Verify renderables/mod.rs exports correct modules
   - Test that feature-gated types are NOT available when feature disabled

3. RUNTIME BEHAVIOR TESTS:
   - Basic rendering works in all combinations
   - Feature-specific functionality works when enabled
   - No panics when feature modules are absent

4. CI MATRIX:
   Create GitHub Actions matrix testing all combinations:
   

5. DOCUMENTATION:
   - Document which features enable which functionality
   - Document any feature interactions or conflicts

LOGGING:
- Log which features are enabled at test startup
- Log any feature-conditional code paths taken

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] All 8 feature combinations compile
- [ ] All 8 combinations pass basic tests
- [ ] CI matrix configured and passing
- [ ] No conditional compilation warnings","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:33.94158072-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:33.94158072-05:00","labels":["ci","infrastructure","testing"]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-ehe","title":"Create E2E Test: Table Rendering Scenarios","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive E2E tests for Table rendering with detailed logging.

## Background
Tables are the most complex renderable with many interacting features: column sizing, row rendering, borders, alignment. E2E tests ensure all features work together.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Simple Table
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_simple_table() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("e2e_simple_table");
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("Name"))
        .add_column(Column::new("Age"))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Alice"), Cell::new("30")]))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Bob"), Cell::new("25")]));
    
    debug!(columns = 2, rows = 2, "Table structure");
    
    let console = Console::new().width(40);
    let output = console.render_to_string(&table);
    
    debug!(output = %output, "Rendered table");
    
    // Verify structure
    assert!(output.contains("Name"), "Missing header");
    assert!(output.contains("Alice"), "Missing data");
    assert!(output.lines().count() >= 4, "Insufficient lines");
    
    info!(lines = output.lines().count(), "Table rendered successfully");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Table with All Features
- Title and caption
- Header and footer
- Multiple box styles
- Styled cells
- Vertical alignment in cells

### Scenario 3: Width Constraints
- Table wider than terminal (must collapse)
- Table narrower than terminal (must expand)
- Columns at minimum width
- Ratio-based column distribution

### Scenario 4: Wide Characters in Cells
- CJK characters
- Emoji
- Mixed ASCII/CJK

### Scenario 5: Multi-line Cells
- Cells with newlines
- Cells that wrap
- Vertical alignment with multi-line

### Scenario 6: Edge Cases
- Empty table
- Single cell
- Very narrow width (force minimum)
- Very wide content

## Logging Requirements
Each test must log:
1. **Setup**: Table structure (columns, rows, options)
2. **Width calculation**: Input widths, available width, result widths
3. **Row rendering**: Cell content, alignment applied
4. **Border drawing**: Box style, characters used
5. **Final output**: Complete rendered string
6. **Timing**: Time for width calc, time for render

## Golden Files
Create golden files for each scenario to detect visual regressions.

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_table.rs
- tests/golden/table_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All table scenarios pass. Width algorithms verified. Logs show calculation details.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:29.700982578-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:29.700982578-05:00","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.880043097-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.007910488-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-eoi","title":"Validate Markdown Module (feature-gated)","description":"Validate the Markdown module (requires "markdown" feature flag) against Python Rich Markdown behavior.

FEATURE FLAG: markdown (requires pulldown-cmark crate)

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. Markdown struct construction
2. Supported Markdown elements
3. Style application for different element types
4. Code block handling (inline and fenced)

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Markdown::new() accepts markdown text
- [ ] Paragraph rendering with proper line breaks
- [ ] Header rendering (h1-h6 with proper styling)
- [ ] Bold/italic/strikethrough inline formatting
- [ ] Inline code with background styling
- [ ] Fenced code blocks (with syntax highlighting if syntax feature enabled)
- [ ] Links (may render as styled text or hyperlinks)
- [ ] Unordered lists with proper bullets
- [ ] Ordered lists with numbers
- [ ] Nested lists
- [ ] Blockquotes with styling
- [ ] Horizontal rules
- [ ] Images (text placeholder or omitted)

EDGE CASES:
- [ ] Empty markdown
- [ ] Deeply nested elements
- [ ] Mixed inline formatting
- [ ] Code blocks without language
- [ ] HTML in markdown (stripped or rendered?)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/markdown.rs
- Python rich/markdown.py for reference","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:08.008977782-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T11:14:08.008977782-05:00","labels":["feature-gated","validation"]}

- Equal or auto-sized column widths
- Column padding/gutters
- Expandable columns","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:32:49.085122393-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T09:44:15.617331007-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T09:44:15.617331007-05:00","close_reason":"Columns renderable implemented with multi-column layout, auto column count, gutter support, equal width mode, alignment options, and 14 passing tests"}
 {"id":"rich_rust-g92","title":"Validate Color System against RICH_SPEC Section 1","description":"# Validate Color System against RICH_SPEC Section 1

## Context
The Color System is foundational to rich_rust. It handles color representation, parsing, palette management, and ANSI code generation.

## Spec Requirements to Validate (from RICH_SPEC.md §1)

### 1.1 Data Structures
- [ ] ColorTriplet: `hex()`, `rgb()`, `normalized()` methods
- [ ] ColorSystem enum: STANDARD (1), EIGHT_BIT (2), TRUECOLOR (3), WINDOWS (4)
- [ ] ColorType enum: DEFAULT (0), STANDARD (1), EIGHT_BIT (2), TRUECOLOR (3), WINDOWS (4)
- [ ] Color struct: name, color_type, number, triplet fields

### 1.2 Color Parsing (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Named colors: "red", "bright_blue" → STANDARD/EIGHT_BIT
- [ ] Hex format: "#FF0000" → TRUECOLOR
- [ ] Color number: "color(196)" → STANDARD if 0-15, EIGHT_BIT if 16-255
- [ ] RGB format: "rgb(255,0,0)" → TRUECOLOR
- [ ] Default: "default" → ColorType::DEFAULT
- [ ] Case-insensitivity
- [ ] Whitespace handling in rgb()
- [ ] **LRU cache (max 1024 entries)** - verify caching exists

### 1.3 Color Palettes
- [ ] STANDARD_PALETTE: 16 colors with exact RGB values
- [ ] EIGHT_BIT_PALETTE: 256 colors (16 standard + 216 cube + 24 grayscale)
- [ ] WINDOWS_PALETTE: 16 colors with exact RGB values
- [ ] 6x6x6 cube formula: `16 + 36 * red_index + 6 * green_index + blue_index`
- [ ] Grayscale ramp: indices 232-255 with values (8,8,8) to (238,238,238)

### 1.4 Color Conversion Algorithms
- [ ] RGB to 8-bit: grayscale detection via HLS saturation < 0.15
- [ ] RGB to Standard: weighted CIE76 color distance formula
- [ ] Verify exact formulas match spec

### 1.5 ANSI Code Generation
- [ ] DEFAULT: ["39"] foreground, ["49"] background
- [ ] STANDARD 0-7: ["30"+n] fg, ["40"+n] bg
- [ ] STANDARD 8-15: ["82"+n] fg, ["92"+n] bg
- [ ] EIGHT_BIT: ["38", "5", "N"] fg, ["48", "5", "N"] bg
- [ ] TRUECOLOR: ["38", "2", "R", "G", "B"] fg, ["48", "2", "R", "G", "B"] bg

## Files to Check
- `src/color.rs` (955 lines)

## Validation Approach
1. Read src/color.rs thoroughly
2. Compare each implementation detail against spec
3. Create tests for any uncovered edge cases
4. Document discrepancies
5. Fix implementation or update spec as needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- All spec requirements validated with tests
- LRU caching verified or implemented
- Color conversion algorithms match exactly
- ANSI code generation matches spec table","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:47.105219826-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:47.105219826-05:00","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-g92","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.645784979-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
